title: Display capacity confused with inventory and fulfillment
prompt: An online merchant selects at most twelve products for a common homepage module. The only capacity is twelve display positions; inventory quantities and fulfillment resources are fixed outside the model. A suggested narrative treats every displayed product as consuming warehouse stock and delivery capacity. Diagnose the capacity conflation and retain the display-set decision.

model_specification.mathematical_objective: maximize expected margin from the displayed set
decision_variables: products included in the homepage module
model_specification.mechanism: The displayed set defines the alternatives from which visitors purchase or exit under the modeled choice probabilities.

fidelity_requirements: No inventory allocation or fulfillment-capacity decision is present
prohibited_inferences: Displaying a product consumes one physical inventory unit
The model allocates warehouse or delivery capacity

evidence_needs.claim: Twelve items fit in the homepage module
